genre_preference.value: dislike for poorly acted dramas, preference for nostalgic content
evidence: "The children were awesome but as adults the choice of the actors stinks."
"I miss the old movies and actors who could act."
"This is the most dribbly slop opera. I mean damn just get on with it."
"Totally not impressed too dribbly. Nothing happens."
"I mean damn just get on with it."
"the season started with them fighting the saviors and the last episode... ended where they started."
"the acting was pitiful. Just lame and a great waste of my time."
"I absolutely hated the way this was filmed."
"Don't like affleck at all. Not an actor. Just gross."
"I had to quit watching this. I read and enjoyed the books very much."
"The photography is lovely. The information is lousy."
"I absolutely hated the way this was filmed."
"Did not like this a big waste of time the acting was extremely bad."
"This is so not even worth spending ten seconds on."

summary: The user has a strong aversion to poorly executed dramas and a preference for nostalgic content. They value production quality highly and prefer straightforward narratives, often expressing frustration with slow pacing and lack of engaging storytelling.
